title: Bounded Worker Pool, Origin Fairness, Cancellation, and Shutdown · Go Semantics Case: Feed Go Channel Ownership
summary: The scheduler launches one goroutine per source, results block after cancellation, one origin occupies all workers, panics leak claims, and shutdown exits before commits settle. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go channel ownership increment in bounded origin-aware ingestion worker pool. Learners progress through prediction, expli…
objectives: Assign producers, closers, receivers, buffers, and terminal states so sends, closes, and drains cannot race or block forever.
Derive global, tenant, origin, database, memory, and queue budgets and enforce the minimum active constraint.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Channel Ownership · Theory Feed Go Channel Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 1999 during a PostgreSQL claim and failover drill. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
